slides.title:
Talk to your data.
Get answers you can trust.
slides.subtitle: Natural-language analytics over your own database — governed by a semantic layer, so the numbers are right.

slides.title: Every business question waits in a queue
bullets: Answers are gated by SQL and a short list of analysts.
BI dashboards answer yesterday's questions, not today's.
Point a raw LLM at the database and it invents columns and joins — confident, and wrong.
So teams either wait, or trust numbers no one checked.

slides.title: Ask in plain English. Governed by your semantic model.
Why the numbers are right

panels.title: A governed semantic layer
panels.subtitle: trustworthy
lines: Honest when it can't answer a cut

slides.caption: The AI can only pick from the metrics and dimensions you define — it cannot invent a column or a join. Every query is checked before it runs.

slides.label: rows leave your database
slides.context: It runs on your own SQLite, Postgres, or MySQL through a portable semantic layer — no data warehouse to migrate to, no data handed to a third party.

slides.title: What your team gets
left: A chart that states the takeaway
The exact numbers, and the table
right: A live map of your metrics & data
Honest notes when a breakdown drops
Chat, or switch the chart type yourself
Works from day one on your model

slides.title: Quality you can see — and watch improve
left: On every change to the engine
right: A chart of quality over time
So you see it going up, not just claimed

slides.title: Where it fits
right: a conversational layer over governed metrics
dbt / Cube-style governance, in plain English

slides.title: Onboarding a new client
bullets: Point it at the semantic model: your tables, metrics, dimensions, joins.
Add verified questions — these both teach the AI and become its evals.
It's live: your team asks questions; you watch the accuracy trend.
Runs in your infrastructure, on the database you already have.

slides.title: Where it's going
bullets: More database engines behind the same semantic layer.
A polished, brandable web front end (React / Next.js).
Deeper analytics: comparisons, trends, and forecasts.

slides.title: Your data, your database, in plain English.
